seo.title: Building Content Generation with LangChain + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build content generation with LangChain + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building content generation applications with LangChain enables powerful LLM-driven creation pipelines, from blog posts to marketing copy. However, risks like hallucinations, brand voice drift, and factual inaccuracies can damage reputation and trust. Vex provides runtime verification to enforce brand consistency, factual accuracy, and editorial standards across your chains.

architectureOverview.heading: Content Generation Architecture with LangChain
architectureOverview.description:
A typical LangChain content generation pipeline starts with a research/retrieval step using RAG to gather brand guidelines, competitor analysis, and factual data. This feeds into a multi-step chain: an outline generator creates structure, a content drafter produces initial copy, and a brand voice enforcer refines tone and style. Finally, an editor chain applies SEO optimization and fact-checking against verified sources.

Agents often coordinate these steps, calling tools for keyword research, plagiarism checks, and style analysis. Sequential chains ensure each step builds on verified outputs, but failure modes like context overflow or inconsistent formatting across steps create brittle pipelines.

Vex wraps critical invoke() calls to verify outputs against brand voice profiles, fact-checking APIs, and editorial rubrics before final assembly.

risks.name: Hallucination in RAG retrieval chains
risks.description: Content drafters invent facts when retrieval misses key brand guidelines or source documents
risks.mitigation: Vex factual accuracy checks against verified sources before chain progression

risks.name: Inconsistent output formatting across chain steps
risks.description: Outline generator uses bullet points but drafter expects JSON, breaking downstream parsing
risks.mitigation: Vex structured output validation ensures consistent schema across chain handoffs

risks.name: Context window overflow in long conversations
risks.description: Brand voice refinement accumulates full article + guidelines, exceeding token limits
risks.mitigation: Vex context summarization and relevance scoring prevents overflow

risks.name: Brand voice drift in iterative refinement
risks.description: Multiple chain passes gradually shift from formal corporate tone to casual blog style
risks.mitigation: Vex continuous brand voice scoring maintains consistency through pipeline

implementation.title: Basic Content Generation Chain
implementation.description: Simple sequential chain for blog post generation with brand context injection
implementation.code:
from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate

from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI

from langchain_core.output_parsers import StrOutputParser


llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini", temperature=0.7)

brand_prompt = ChatPromptTemplate.from_template(
    """You are writing for {brand_name}. Key guidelines:

{brand_guidelines}

Topic: {topic}

Write a {length} article following these guidelines exactly:"""
)

content_chain = brand_prompt | llm | StrOutputParser()

result = content_chain.invoke({
    "brand_name": "TechCorp",
    "brand_guidelines": "Professional tone. Use active voice. 2nd person addressing CTOs.",
    "topic": "AI Agent Reliability",
    "length": "800-word"
})

print(result)

implementation.title: RAG-Enhanced Research Chain
implementation.description: Retrieval chain pulls competitor analysis and industry stats for factual grounding
implementation.code:
from langchain_community.vectorstores import FAISS

from langchain_openai import OpenAIEmbeddings

from langchain_text_splitters import CharacterTextSplitter


# Sample research docs
docs = ["Competitor X uses RAG...", "Industry avg hallucination rate 23%..."]

text_splitter = CharacterTextSplitter(chunk_size=1000, chunk_overlap=0)
splits = text_splitter.create_documents(docs)

vectorstore = FAISS.from_documents(splits, OpenAIEmbeddings())
retriever = vectorstore.as_retriever()

research_template = ChatPromptTemplate.from_template(
    """Research for article on {topic}. Use only these sources:

{context}

Key findings (bullets):"""
)

research_chain = ({"context": retriever, "topic": RunnablePassthrough()}
                 | research_template
                 | llm
                 | StrOutputParser())

vexIntegration.title: Wrap Chain Invoke with Vex Verify
vexIntegration.description: Basic integration verifying brand consistency and factual accuracy on content output
vexIntegration.code:
import vex

from langchain_core.runnables import RunnablePassthrough


# Configure Vex verifier for content generation
verifier = vex.Verifier(
    name="content-generation",
    checks=[
        vex.Check.brand_voice(model="vex-brand-v1", threshold=0.85),
        vex.Check.factual_accuracy(sources=["company-wiki", "verified-stats"]),
        vex.Check.seo_compliance(keywords=["llm", "reliability", "guardrails"])
    ]
)


# Wrap the content chain
@verifier.wrap
async def verified_content_chain(inputs):
    result = await content_chain.ainvoke(inputs)
    return result


# Usage with verification
try:
    verified_output = await verified_content_chain({
        "topic": "AI Content Safety",
        "brand_name": "Vex"
    })
    print("✅ Verified content:", verified_output)
except vex.VerificationError as e:
    print("❌ Blocked:", e.failures)

vexIntegration.title: Multi-Step Chain Verification
vexIntegration.description: Verify each chain step with progressive checks building context assurance
vexIntegration.code:
import vex


research_verifier = vex.Verifier(
    "research-step",
    checks=[vex.Check.relevance(threshold=0.9)]
)

draft_verifier = vex.Verifier(
    "draft-step",
    checks=[
        vex.Check.factual_accuracy(),
        vex.Check.length(min=800, max=1200)
    ]
)

final_verifier = vex.Verifier(
    "final-content",
    checks=[
        vex.Check.brand_consistency(profile="vex-corporate"),
        vex.Check.originality(threshold=0.95),
        vex.Check.seo(keywords=["ai reliability"])
    ]
)


@research_verifier.wrap
async def research_step(topic): return await research_chain.ainvoke(topic)

@draft_verifier.wrap
async def draft_step(research): return await draft_chain.ainvoke(research)

@final_verifier.wrap
async def finalize(draft): return await editor_chain.ainvoke(draft)


# Pipeline execution
research = await research_step({"topic": "LLM Guardrails"})
draft = await draft_step(research)
final = await finalize(draft)
